name: Stanford University
notes: One of the premier programs in the country for both genders. Extremely competitive recruiting. Top international and domestic talent. Financial aid is very strong.

name: University of North Carolina
notes: One of the most storied soccer programs in the country. Women's program has won 22 national championships. Men's program is consistently top-10. Highly selective.

name: Harvard University
notes: Ivy League — no athletic scholarships. Requires exceptional academics AND athletic profile. Families earning under $85k pay nothing.

name: Yale University
notes: Ivy League. Strong soccer tradition for both programs. Highly selective.

name: Princeton University
notes: Ivy League. Elite academics and athletics. Very selective.

name: University of Pennsylvania
notes: Ivy League. Strong soccer program. Philadelphia location.

name: Dartmouth College
notes: Ivy League. Strong academics and athletics.

name: Brown University
notes: Ivy League. Open curriculum. Excellent soccer program.

name: Columbia University
notes: Ivy League. NYC location. Highly selective.

name: Cornell University
notes: Ivy League. Largest Ivy. Strong STEM and liberal arts. Competitive soccer programs.

name: University of Michigan
notes: Strong Big Ten program. Excellent academics. Men's program consistently ranks nationally.

name: Ohio State University
notes: Strong Big Ten soccer. Large campus, great resources. Competitive recruiting.

name: UCLA
notes: Elite program. Men's won 1 NCAA title, Women's are perennial contenders. Very selective.

name: University of Virginia
notes: Men's program is an NCAA powerhouse with multiple national titles. Women's program is strong ACC contender.

name: Northwestern University
notes: Highly selective private school in the Big Ten. Strong academics and athletic program.

name: Duke University
notes: Highly selective. Elite soccer tradition. Both programs consistently ranked nationally.

name: Wake Forest University
notes: Strong ACC soccer. Men's program nationally ranked. Good academics and athletics balance.

name: Boston College
notes: Strong ACC program. Good academics. Strong soccer tradition for both programs.

name: University of Texas
notes: Strong SEC women's program. No men's soccer program.

name: University of Florida
notes: Strong SEC women's program. No men's soccer.

name: University of Southern California
notes: Elite women's program. Multiple NCAA semifinal appearances. No men's soccer.

name: University of Wisconsin
notes: Good Big Ten program. Solid academics. Less cutthroat than elite schools. Good value.

name: Penn State University
notes: Strong Big Ten program. Women's team is a consistent Big Ten contender. Good value.

name: University of Colorado
notes: Solid Big 12 women's program. No men's soccer.

name: University of Oregon
notes: Developing Big Ten women's program. No men's soccer.

name: Vanderbilt University
notes: Elite academics with SEC athletics. Women's only. No men's soccer.

name: Rice University
notes: Highly selective. Women's only. No men's soccer.

name: Boston University
notes: Good Patriot League program. Strong academics. Urban Boston setting.

name: University of Denver
notes: Good mid-major program. Men's program has been nationally ranked. More accessible than Power Five.

name: Marquette University
notes: Big East soccer. More accessible than top programs. Good value for a private school.

name: University of Connecticut
notes: Good Big East program. Solid value public option.

name: Rutgers University
notes: Big Ten soccer. Men's program is one of the oldest in the country. Good value.

name: University of Maryland
notes: Strong Big Ten program. Men's program is a national powerhouse. Excellent value for in-state.

name: University of South Carolina
notes: SEC women's program. Good value. No men's soccer.

name: Auburn University
notes: SEC women's program. Good value. No men's soccer.

name: Clemson University
notes: Strong ACC program. Men's program has won 3 NCAA titles. Good academics and athletics balance.

name: Virginia Tech
notes: Good ACC option. More accessible than top ACC schools. Solid value.

name: University of Louisville
notes: ACC soccer. Men's program has been nationally ranked. Good value and more accessible.

name: George Mason University
notes: Mid-major D1. Good value. Accessible. Strong club soccer culture in the DC metro area.

name: University of Rhode Island
notes: Mid-major. Good value. More accessible for the right profile.

name: University of New Hampshire
notes: Mid-major. Good option for New England players. Accessible.

name: Merrimack College
notes: D1 transition program. Accessible. Northeast Conference.

name: Georgetown University
notes: Elite Big East program. Men's program is a national powerhouse with 2 NCAA titles. Strong academics.

name: Notre Dame
notes: Strong ACC program. Men's program has won 1 NCAA title. Prestigious academics. Highly selective.

name: Syracuse University
notes: ACC soccer. More accessible than elite ACC schools. Good recruiting opportunities.

name: Indiana University
notes: One of the most storied men's programs in US history (8 NCAA titles). Women's program also strong. Big Ten.

name: University of Washington
notes: Strong Big Ten program. Good value for in-state students. Pacific Northwest recruiting hub.

name: University of Portland
notes: National soccer powerhouse. Women's program won 2 NCAA titles. Men's has been nationally ranked. Small school, close-knit team culture.

name: Santa Clara University
notes: Soccer-centric WCC school. Both programs have been ranked nationally. Silicon Valley location.

name: Creighton University
notes: Big East soccer school. Men's program has won 2 NCAA titles. More affordable than other Big East private schools.

name: North Carolina State University
notes: ACC soccer. Excellent in-state value. Both programs competing at a high level.

name: University of Michigan State
notes: Big Ten program. Good value for in-state. More accessible than Michigan.

name: University of Minnesota
notes: Big Ten program. Good in-state value. Urban campus in Minneapolis.

name: SMU (Southern Methodist University)
notes: Newly in ACC. Men's and women's programs both competitive. Dallas location with strong Texas recruiting ties.

name: University of California, Berkeley
notes: Highly selective public university. Strong academics. Both soccer programs are competitive at the Big Ten level.

name: Florida State University
notes: Women's program is a perennial national title contender with 11 NCAA championships. Men's program also consistently ranked. Excellent in-state value.

name: University of Tennessee
notes: Strong SEC women's program. No men's soccer.

name: University of Georgia
notes: SEC women's program. Good value for in-state. No men's soccer.

name: University of Alabama
notes: SEC women's program. Good value. No men's soccer.

name: University of Arkansas
notes: SEC women's program. Affordable in-state option. No men's soccer.

name: TCU (Texas Christian University)
notes: Big 12 program. Both men's and women's programs are nationally competitive. Strong Texas recruiting base.

name: West Virginia University
notes: Big 12 program. More accessible. Good value especially in-state.

name: Oklahoma State University
notes: Big 12 women's program. No men's soccer. Good in-state value.

name: Fordham University
notes: NYC area. Atlantic 10. Good academic profile. Solid soccer program.

name: Davidson College
notes: Small liberal arts school with strong athletics. Atlantic 10. Selective. Good soccer program.

name: University of San Diego
notes: WCC soccer. Beautiful San Diego campus. Good academic profile.

name: Gonzaga University
notes: WCC program. Strong academics. Accessible for a private school.

name: Loyola Marymount University
notes: WCC soccer in LA. Good soccer culture. More accessible than UCLA/USC.

name: UC Davis
notes: UC system. Good academics. Big West conference. Solid mid-major option.

name: Cal Poly San Luis Obispo
notes: Strong academics for a polytechnic. Big West. Excellent in-state value.

name: Missouri University
notes: SEC women's program. Good value. No men's soccer.

name: University of Kentucky
notes: SEC women's program. Very accessible. Good in-state value. No men's soccer.

name: University of Mississippi
notes: SEC women's program. Very accessible. Good in-state value. No men's soccer.

name: Central Connecticut State University
notes: D1 NEC. Very accessible. Good value for in-state Connecticut students.

name: Grand Canyon University
notes: WAC program. Very accessible and affordable. Phoenix metro area.

name: Sacred Heart University
notes: NEC D1. Soccer focused. Accessible. Connecticut location.

name: Stonehill College
notes: D2 NE10. Accessible. Good soccer culture in New England.

name: University of Tampa
notes: Strong D2 program. Men's program has won 3 DII national titles. Competitive and warm weather.

name: Rollins College
notes: Florida D2 school. Smaller class sizes. Competitive SSC conference. Good academics for D2.

name: Limestone University
notes: D2 soccer powerhouse. Men's program has won 3 DII national titles. High international percentage. Very accessible.

name: Lynn University
notes: D2 SSC. Very high international percentage. Men's program nationally ranked in D2. Boca Raton location.

name: Barry University
notes: D2 SSC. Very high international percentage. Accessible. Miami area location.

name: West Chester University
notes: Strong D2 PSAC program. Men's program nationally ranked. Excellent in-state value.

name: Kutztown University
notes: D2 PSAC. Very accessible. Strong in-state value in Pennsylvania.

name: Colorado School of Mines
notes: Unique STEM-focused D2 school. Strong academics. Competitive RMAC soccer. Good for athletes who are strong in engineering/sciences.

name: Regis University
notes: D2 RMAC in Denver. Accessible. Good soccer culture in Colorado.

name: Tiffin University
notes: D2 G-MAC. High international percentage. Very affordable. Accessible.

name: Franklin Pierce University
notes: D2 NE10. Very accessible. Small school atmosphere. New England.

name: Amherst College
notes: Top NESCAC liberal arts. No athletic scholarships but excellent need-based aid. Highly selective. Elite academic and soccer environment.

name: Williams College
notes: Consistently ranked #1 liberal arts college. No athletic scholarships. Highly selective. Elite NESCAC soccer.

name: Middlebury College
notes: Top NESCAC. Known for language programs and outdoor culture. Strong soccer. No athletic scholarships.

name: Bowdoin College
notes: Top NESCAC. Highly selective. Beautiful Maine campus. Strong soccer tradition.

name: Wesleyan University
notes: NESCAC. Strong academics and arts culture. Competitive soccer.

name: Colby College
notes: NESCAC. Highly selective. Strong academic profile. Maine campus.

name: Tufts University
notes: NESCAC. Near Boston. Strong international relations and pre-med culture. Excellent academics and soccer.

name: Bates College
notes: NESCAC. Highly selective. No SAT/ACT required. Strong soccer program.

name: Trinity College
notes: NESCAC. More accessible than top NESCAC schools. Hartford location.

name: Hamilton College
notes: NESCAC. Strong academics. Writing-focused curriculum. Highly selective.

name: Swarthmore College
notes: Top liberal arts. Highly selective. Centennial Conference. Near Philadelphia.

name: Haverford College
notes: Top liberal arts. Bico with Bryn Mawr. Highly selective. Centennial Conference.

name: Washington University in St. Louis
notes: Top D3 program. Multiple NCAA soccer championships. Highly selective. Research university with strong pre-med and business.

name: Emory University
notes: Top D3 UAA. Strong pre-med. Highly selective. Atlanta location. Strong soccer program.

name: University of Chicago
notes: Highly selective research university. D3 UAA. Rigorous academics. Strong soccer program.

name: Johns Hopkins University
notes: Top research university. Highly selective. D3 Centennial. Strong pre-med culture. Excellent soccer.

name: Pomona College
notes: Top Claremont Consortium liberal arts. Highly selective. SCIAC. California location.

name: Claremont-McKenna College
notes: Claremont Consortium. Economics and government focused. Plays as CMS (Claremont-Mudd-Scripps) in athletics. Highly selective.

name: Kenyon College
notes: Liberal arts. Famous for writing programs. NCAC. More accessible than NESCAC top schools.

name: Oberlin College
notes: Arts and sciences focused. NCAC. Strong progressive culture. Good soccer program.

name: Denison University
notes: NCAC liberal arts. Good soccer program. More accessible than top-tier D3.

name: Brandeis University
notes: Research university. UAA. Near Boston. Strong academics and athletics.

name: Rochester Institute of Technology
notes: STEM-focused. Liberty League. More accessible. Good for soccer players interested in engineering/technology.

name: Case Western Reserve University
notes: Top research university in D3. UAA. Highly selective. Strong STEM focus. Good soccer program.

name: Carnegie Mellon University
notes: Elite research and engineering university. D3 UAA. Highly selective. Great for tech-minded athletes.

name: NYU (New York University)
notes: NYC location. Highly selective. D3 UAA. Strong soccer program. High international percentage.

name: Ithaca College
notes: Communications and arts focused. Liberty League. More accessible. Competitive D3 soccer.

name: Gettysburg College
notes: Centennial Conference. Liberal arts. Competitive D3 soccer in a strong conference.

name: Dickinson College
notes: Centennial Conference. Liberal arts. Sustainability-focused. Accessible compared to top D3.

name: Franklin & Marshall College
notes: Centennial Conference. Strong academics. Good D3 soccer program in a competitive conference.

name: Muhlenberg College
notes: Centennial Conference. Theater and pre-med culture. More accessible. Competitive soccer.
